name: SafetyConstraintProjector envelope bounds
aim: Project() output must be within integratedEnvelope [lo, up] for any input

threshold.rationale: CRITICAL — no action may exceed the safety envelope

on_exceed: CRITICAL: Action exceeds physical safety envelope → actuator operates in unsafe regime → hardware damage risk

answered_questions.what_was_tested: 50000 inputs × 4 dimensions = 200000 bound checks with action magnitudes up to ±190
answered_questions.why_this_threshold: integratedEnvelope is the hard safety clamp. The gradient descent may not converge, but the envelope MUST clamp
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: Action outside envelope → actuator commanded beyond physical limits → mechanical/electrical failure
answered_questions.how_interface_was_verified: Compute exact upper/lower per dimension from integratedEnvelope formula, check every output element

name: SafetyConstraintProjector correction effectiveness
aim: Projector must reduce action magnitude for >99% of unsafe inputs

threshold.rationale: Projector that doesn't correct is a no-op — defeats safety purpose

on_exceed: Projector passes unsafe actions through unchanged → safety layer is decorative, not functional

answered_questions.what_was_tested: 10000 inputs with action magnitude 20+ (far beyond envelope ~3.7)
answered_questions.why_this_threshold: Input at 20+ vs envelope at ~3.7 → projector must reduce. 99% allows tiny tolerance for edge cases where gradient is trapped
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: Safety projector passes dangerous actions unchanged → actuator operates in unsafe region → damage
answered_questions.how_interface_was_verified: Compare ‖input action‖ vs ‖projected action‖, verify output norm < input norm

name: SafetyConstraintProjector output finite
aim: Project() must never return NaN/Inf in Action, ViolationNorm, or ConstraintCost

threshold.rationale: CRITICAL — NaN in action vector → undefined actuator behavior

on_exceed: CRITICAL: NaN/Inf in projected action → actuator receives undefined command → system crash or hardware damage

answered_questions.what_was_tested: 20000 adversarial inputs with action range [-500, 499], risk [0, 1], capPress [0, 1]
answered_questions.why_this_threshold: NaN/Inf propagates through the entire actuator chain — any single occurrence is catastrophic
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: NaN action → actuator produces undefined behavior → system enters unknown state
answered_questions.how_interface_was_verified: Check all output fields (Action[], ViolationNorm, ConstraintCost) for IsNaN/IsInf

name: Full autopilot pipeline end-to-end tick latency
aim: MPC→Safety→Rollout→Predictor→ID pipeline must complete within SLA: p50<1ms p95<3ms p99<8ms p100<20ms

threshold.rationale: Control loop at 125Hz = 8ms budget per pipeline tick

on_exceed: Pipeline too slow → control frequency drops → actuator corrections delayed → system drifts

answered_questions.what_was_tested: Full pipeline across 1000 ticks with oscillating arrival (3±4) and infraLoad (0.2±0.1)
answered_questions.why_this_threshold: The autopilot tick includes MPC SA optimization (50 iters × 8 scenarios), safety evaluation, rollout, predictor, and identification — all must fit in <8ms at p99
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: Autopilot cannot maintain >100Hz control frequency → delayed corrections → oscillation/instability
answered_questions.how_interface_was_verified: Wall-clock measurement of each Tick() call with varying arrival and infra load

name: Pipeline output no NaN/Inf corruption
aim: RuntimeOrchestrator.Tick must never produce NaN/Inf in any telemetry field

threshold.rationale: Any NaN/Inf in telemetry corrupts all downstream consumers

on_exceed: NaN/Inf in telemetry → dashboard shows garbage → operator loses situational awareness → bad decisions

answered_questions.why_this_threshold: NaN/Inf propagates: one bad value corrupts all downstream computations (MPC cost, safety energy, etc.)
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: NaN enters feedback loop → MPC produces NaN cost → simulated annealing accepts anything → system diverges
answered_questions.how_interface_was_verified: Check every RuntimeTelemetry field for math.IsNaN and math.IsInf after each Tick() call

name: MPC plan length always matches horizon
aim: MPCOptimiser.Optimise must return exactly Horizon-length sequence

threshold.rationale: Short plan → truncated safety trajectory → undetected risk

on_exceed: MPC returns short plan → SafetyEngine.ShouldOverrideProb operates on truncated trajectory → misses future hazards

answered_questions.why_this_threshold: Safety evaluation depends on len(plan)==Horizon. Any mismatch silently truncates the safety check
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: Safety engine evaluates fewer future states than expected → blind spot in predictive safety
answered_questions.how_interface_was_verified: Call Optimise with various prevSeq lengths, check len(result)==Horizon

name: CoalescingActuator dispatch + feedback contract
aim: Every dispatched active directive must produce feedback with Success=true

threshold.rationale: LogOnlyBackend never errors → all feedback must show Success=true

on_exceed: Actuator feedback shows failures for noop backend → feedback channel logic broken → control loop cannot confirm actuation

answered_questions.why_this_threshold: LogOnlyBackend.Execute always returns nil → 0 failures expected
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: Control loop thinks actuation failed → triggers fallback/retry paths unnecessarily
answered_questions.how_interface_was_verified: Dispatch → drain Feedback() channel → verify each ActuationResult.Success==true

name: Coalescer map-overwrite contract
aim: 10 rapid dispatches for same service must coalesce — only latest executes

threshold.rationale: Map overwrite ensures only latest pending directive survives per service key

on_exceed: Stale directives execute alongside fresh ones → actuator sends contradictory commands → system oscillation

answered_questions.what_was_tested: 10 rapid dispatches for same service key, 1 actual executions
answered_questions.why_this_threshold: Map-coalesce contract: pending[serviceID] = latest snapshot. Worker drains once → executes only the latest
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: Stale scale factors reach backend → actuator oscillates between old and new targets
answered_questions.how_interface_was_verified: Tracking backend records every Execute call; verify last executed scale >= 9.0

name: RouterBackend correct service routing
aim: Directives must be routed to service-specific backends; unknown services go to default

threshold.rationale: Each service must reach exactly the backend registered via AddRoute

on_exceed: Directive sent to wrong backend → wrong subsystem actuated → cascading control failure

answered_questions.what_was_tested: 4 directives across 3 services (2 explicitly routed, 1 default)
answered_questions.why_this_threshold: Routing is deterministic — any misroute is a code defect in RouterBackend.Execute
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: Scaling commands sent to wrong microservice → capacity mismatch → instability
answered_questions.how_interface_was_verified: Register 2 service-specific backends + default, execute snaps, verify which backend received each

name: Actuator dispatch-to-feedback latency SLA
aim: CoalescingActuator dispatch→feedback latency: p50<5ms p95<15ms p99<30ms

threshold.rationale: Real-time control loop requires <30ms actuator response at p99

on_exceed: Actuator feedback arrives late → control loop operates on stale confirmation → timing drift

answered_questions.what_was_tested: Dispatch-to-feedback latency across 200 sequential directives with LogOnlyBackend
answered_questions.why_this_threshold: Control loop runs at ~33Hz (30ms). Actuator feedback must arrive within 1 cycle
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: Control loop starts next tick before knowing if previous actuation succeeded
answered_questions.how_interface_was_verified: Measure wall-clock time from Dispatch() to Feedback() receipt per directive

name: QueueBackend worker scaling correctness
aim: QueueBackend must scale workers as round(current*scaleFactor), clamped to minWorkers=1

threshold.rationale: Worker count must follow round(current*scale) with minWorkers=1 floor

on_exceed: Worker scaling formula wrong → capacity mismatch → over/under-provisioned services

answered_questions.why_this_threshold: Scaling is deterministic arithmetic — any deviation is a code defect
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: Backend provisions wrong number of workers → service either overloaded or wasting resources
answered_questions.how_interface_was_verified: Execute with known scaleFactor, check WorkerCount() against expected

name: Feedback backpressure behavior documentation
aim: Document feedback drop rate when buffer is full (bufSize=1, N=50 dispatches)

threshold.rationale: At least 1 feedback must arrive even under extreme backpressure

on_exceed: Zero feedbacks received = feedback channel completely broken, not just backpressure

answered_questions.what_was_tested: 50 rapid dispatches with feedback buffer=1, 1 received
answered_questions.why_this_threshold: With buf=1, most feedbacks are dropped by the select-default pattern. >=1 proves the channel works at all
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: Feedback channel completely dead — control loop has zero actuation confirmation
answered_questions.how_interface_was_verified: Dispatch N directives, drain Feedback(), count what gets through

name: SafetyEngine EmergencyOverride fires on unsafe trajectory
aim: EmergencyOverride must trigger when all trajectory states exceed safety limits

threshold.rationale: CRITICAL — zero tolerance for missed unsafe detection

on_exceed: CRITICAL: Unsafe trajectory passes without safety override → system runs in dangerous state → compounding damage

answered_questions.what_was_tested: EmergencyOverride across 1000 trajectories with backlog [200, 5200], all exceeding BaseMaxBacklog=100
answered_questions.why_this_threshold: backlog >> BaseMaxBacklog in every step → PredictiveSafe must return false → override must trigger
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: Safety engine fails to detect blatantly unsafe trajectories → system crashes without intervention
answered_questions.how_interface_was_verified: Build trajectory with backlog=200+(i*5)+(j*10), verify EmergencyOverride returns true

name: PredictiveSafe no false alarms on benign trajectories
aim: PredictiveSafe must return true when backlog<<100 and latency<<50

threshold.rationale: Safe trajectories must not trigger override — false alarms waste capacity

on_exceed: False alarms → system enters unnecessary emergency mode → capacity waste + operator alert fatigue

answered_questions.what_was_tested: PredictiveSafe across 1000 benign trajectories with backlog [0, 10]
answered_questions.why_this_threshold: backlog 0-10 vs limit 100 → well within safe zone. Any alarm is a false positive
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: System triggers emergency override during normal operation → unnecessary capacity spikes
answered_questions.how_interface_was_verified: Build trajectory with backlog<<100, latency<<50, verify PredictiveSafe returns true

name: RolloutController capacity ramp bounded
aim: Each Step must limit CapacityActive change to ramp_rate * Dt * pressure_gain

threshold.rationale: Max ramp per step = 1.250 (CapRampUpEmergency * Dt * max_pressure_mult)

on_exceed: Capacity jumps beyond ramp rate → infrastructure shock (too many containers spawned at once)

answered_questions.what_was_tested: 5000 rollout steps with oscillating capacity targets [1, 19]
answered_questions.why_this_threshold: rampCap clamps step to ±rate*Dt. max rate=5*0.1*2.5=1.250. Any larger jump is a code defect
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: Infrastructure receives sudden capacity change → cold start latency spike → service degradation
answered_questions.how_interface_was_verified: Call Step() N times, check |ΔCapacityActive| <= maxRamp per step

name: GovernanceMode transition correctness
aim: GovernanceMode must follow: Normal < DegradedBacklog < Degraded < EmergencyBacklog < Emergency
on_exceed: Wrong governance mode → wrong ramp rate (normal vs emergency) → capacity response too slow or too fast

answered_questions.what_was_tested: 7 backlog levels against mode thresholds (DegradedBacklog=50, EmergencyBacklog=100)
answered_questions.why_this_threshold: Mode transitions are boundary checks — exact. Any wrong classification is a logic error
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: Emergency capacity ramp applied during normal operation or vice versa
answered_questions.how_interface_was_verified: Step() with known backlog, check resulting RolloutState.Mode

name: Supervisor ShouldRecompute determinism
aim: Same PlantState + fresh Supervisor must produce same ShouldRecompute result

threshold.rationale: Same input → same output for fresh Supervisor instance

on_exceed: Non-deterministic supervisor → recomputation timing unpredictable → control loop scheduling unreliable

answered_questions.why_this_threshold: Supervisor.ShouldRecompute is pure function of PlantState + Supervisor params — must be deterministic for fresh instance
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: MPC recomputation triggers at random → control quality degrades unpredictably
answered_questions.how_interface_was_verified: Create fresh Supervisor, call ShouldRecompute(same state) N times, verify all return same value

name: RuntimeOrchestrator.Tick latency SLA
aim: Single Tick() must complete within p50<2ms p95<5ms p99<10ms

threshold.rationale: Autopilot runs at 100Hz → 10ms budget per tick

on_exceed: Tick exceeds budget → autopilot frequency drops → control latency increases → system drift

answered_questions.what_was_tested: 500 sequential Tick() calls measuring wall-clock latency
answered_questions.why_this_threshold: 100Hz autopilot loop = 10ms budget. MPC SA optimization + safety + rollout must fit
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: Autopilot cannot maintain target control frequency → delayed corrections → instability
answered_questions.how_interface_was_verified: Wall-clock measurement of each Tick() call duration
